zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice C. Irene Tresenriter於1850年3月出生在美國印第安納州弗洛伊德格奧智托 堂史普格奧智托時，她的父親John A Tresenriter Jr33歲，母親Catharine Ann Woolf33歲。她於1866年12月21日與James M. Cartwright在美國印第安納州哈里森結婚。他們至少是5個女兒的父母。她於1900年住在美國阿肯色州波爾克且於1920年住在美國奧克拉荷馬州姆斯可歐吉。

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

nameMeanings.name: Irene
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Greek eirēnē ‘peace’ it was borne in Greek mythology by a minor goddess who personified peace, and by a Byzantine empress ( 752–803 ). The name was taken up in the English-speaking world at the end of the 19th century, and became popular in the 20th, partly as a result of being used as the name of a character in John Galsworthy's The Forsyte Saga ( 1922 ). It was formerly pronounced in three syllables, as in Greek, but is now thoroughly naturalized as an English name and usually pronounced as two syllables.

historicEvents.description: Historical Boundaries 1851: Pemiscot was created from New Madrid County
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: The Assassination of James Garfield
historicEvents.description: Garfield was shot twice by Charles J. Guitea at Railroad Station in Washington, D.C. on July 2, 1881. After eleven weeks of intensive and other care Garfield died in Elberon, New Jersey, the second of four presidents to be assassinated, following Abraham Lincoln.

The Oklahoma Land Run on April 22, 1889, was the first land rush, or land opened for settlement on a first-come basis, opened to the Unassigned Lands. The land rush lured approximately 50,000 people, saddled with their fastest horses, looking to claim their piece of the newly available two million acres. The requirements included the settler to live and improve on their 160 acres for five years in order to receive the title. Choice land tempted people to hide out and get an early lead on their claim. These people became known as “sooners.” It is estimated that eleven thousand homesteads were claimed. Oklahoma Historical Society - Land Run of 1889

historicEvents.title: The Girl Scouts
historicEvents.description: Like the Boy Scouts of America, The Girl Scouts is a youth organization for girls in the United States. Its purpose is to prepare girls to empower themselves and by acquiring practical skills.

historicEvents.title: The Green Corn Rebellion
historicEvents.description: A farmer’s revolt known as the Green Corn Rebellion resulted due to frustration toward landowners and local authorities. A small group of wealthy landowners obtained property by fraudulent means which forced many Oklahoma farmers into a tenancy in 1917. Many farmers joined the Working Class Union who became hostile toward county officials. Hundreds of men gathered on the farm of John Spears in Sasakwa where they planned to march to Washington to repeal the draft act and end the war. Their plan included eating green corn and beef along the way, which gave the rebellion its name. An informer alerted authorities and their effort was halted as several groups collided with the rebels, firing shots into the air. The men scattered, three were killed, over 400 were arrested, and 150 were convicted and received federal prison sentences.

historicEvents.title: Comanche Code Talkers
historicEvents.description: Many Native Americans from Oklahoma were once again employed as code talkers during WWII to create a code impenetrable by enemies. Rather than Choctaw, a Comanche-language code was developed. Several of these men were sent to invade Normandy to send messages. None of the men were killed and the Comanche code was never broken.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice C. Irene Tresenriter was born in March 1850, in Georgetown, Georgetown Township, Floyd, Indiana, United States, her father, John A Tresenriter Jr, was 33 and her mother, Catharine Ann Woolf, was 33. She married James M. Cartwright on 21 December 1866, in Harrison, Indiana, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 daughters. She lived in Polk, Arkansas, United States in 1900 and Muskogee, Oklahoma, United States in 1920.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice C. Irene Tresenriterが1850年3月にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州フロイドジョージェトウン トウンシュイップジョージェトウンで生まれた時，父John A Tresenriter Jrは33歳，母Catharine Ann Woolfは33歳であった。1866年12月21日にアメリカ合衆国インディアナ州ハーリソンでJames M. Cartwrightと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1900年にはアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ポルクに，1920年にはアメリカ合衆国オクラホマ州ムスコジェに住んでいた。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice C. Irene Tresenriter(이)가 1850년 3월 미국 인디애나 주 플로이드 조짓운 타운시프 조짓운에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John A Tresenriter Jr(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Catharine Ann Woolf(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1866년 12월 21일 미국 인디애나 주 해리슨에서 James M. Cartwright(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1900년도에 미국 아칸소 주 폴크에서, 1920년도에 미국 오클라호마 주 무스코그이에서 살았다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice C. Irene Tresenriter nasceu em março de 1850, em Georgetown, Georgetown Township, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, John A Tresenriter Jr, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Catharine Ann Woolf, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com James M. Cartwright em 21 de dezembro de 1866, em Harrison, Indiana, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhas. Ela viveu em Polk, Arcansas, Estados Unidos da América em 1900 e em Muskogee, Oclaoma, Estados Unidos da América em 1920.

ru.Georgetown, Floyd, Indiana, United State: джеоргетоун, джеоргетоун тоуншип, флоид, Индиана, Соединенные штаты

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice C. Irene Tresenriter родилась марта 1850 г. в местности джеоргетоун, джеоргетоун тоуншип, флоид, Индиана, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени John A Tresenriter Jr было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Catharine Ann Woolf, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James M. Cartwright 21 декабря 1866 г. в местности харрисон, Индиана, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 дочерей. Она жила в местности полк, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1900 году и в местности мускоги, Оклахома, Соединенные штаты в 1920 году.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice C. Irene Tresenriter nació en marzo de 1850, en Georgetown, Georgetown Township, Floyd, Indiana, Estados Unidos, su papá, John A Tresenriter Jr, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Catharine Ann Woolf, tenía 33 años. Se casó con James M. Cartwright el 21 de diciembre de 1866, en Harrison, Indiana, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijas. Vivió en Polk, Arkansas, Estados Unidos en 1900 y en Muskogee, Oklahoma, Estados Unidos en 1920.
